_note: Tool (function) configs for the Lexora Hume EVI agent. UNLIKE ElevenLabs, these have NO webhook URL: Hume delivers tool calls over the EVI WebSocket and the browser handles them (app/(demo)/components/HumeWidget.tsx -> onToolCall), which forwards to POST /api/functions/hume?tenant=lexora&fn=<tool name> and returns the result to Hume. Therefore the tool `name` MUST exactly match a handler in lib/functions/handlers.ts (createCaseIntake | saveInjuryInformation | emotionAnalysis | bookConsultation) because the widget routes by name. HOW TO CREATE — Dashboard: platform.hume.ai -> EVI -> Tools -> New tool, paste `name`, `description`, and the `parameters` object below (the UI accepts JSON Schema). API: POST https://api.hume.ai/v0/evi/tools with header `X-Hume-Api-Key: $HUME_API_KEY`; the API requires `parameters` to be a STRINGIFIED JSON Schema, so JSON.stringify(the parameters object shown here). After creating the tools, attach them to an EVI Config (EVI -> Configs), set the system prompt from lib/tenants/lexora.ts (agentPrompt), ENABLE the prosody/emotion model, and copy the Config ID into NEXT_PUBLIC_HUME_CONFIG_ID. The emotionAnalysis tool is optional for the agent — HumeWidget already auto-sends caller emotion from prosody scores — but it's included per the PDF so the agent can also log a reading.

tools.name: createCaseIntake
tools.description: Open a new case file as soon as you have the caller's name. Call once near the start of the conversation. Returns a leadId you must reuse in the later tools.
tools.fallback_content: I'm having trouble opening your case file right now, but I've noted your details and we'll follow up.

accidentType.description: Type of accident, e.g. car accident, slip and fall, workplace injury.

accidentDate.description: When the accident happened, e.g. a date or 'last Tuesday'.

tools.name: saveInjuryInformation
tools.description: Save accident and injury details as you learn them. Pass the leadId returned by createCaseIntake.
tools.fallback_content: I couldn't save that just now, but I've made a note of it.

leadId.description: The case id returned by createCaseIntake.

treatment.description: Any medical treatment received so far.

atFault.description: Who was at fault, if the caller knows.

otherParty.description: The other party involved, if any (e.g. another driver, a business).

tools.name: emotionAnalysis
tools.description: Record the caller's emotional state and overall sentiment. The web app also captures this automatically from voice prosody; call this only if you want to log an explicit reading. Pass the leadId when you have one.

leadId.description: The case id returned by createCaseIntake.

dominantEmotion.description: The caller's dominant emotion, e.g. Distress, Anxiety, Calmness, Relief.

intensity.description: Strength of the dominant emotion, from 0 to 1.

tools.name: bookConsultation
tools.description: Book a consultation with an attorney when the caller is ready. Pass the leadId from createCaseIntake plus the preferred date and time.
tools.fallback_content: I'm having trouble booking that slot right now — our team will call you to confirm a time.

leadId.description: The case id returned by createCaseIntake.

time.description: Preferred consultation time, e.g. 14:00 or '2 PM'.

config_hint._note: When creating the EVI Config that uses these tools, mirror these settings.
config_hint.name: Lexora AI — Legal Intake
config_hint.after_creation: Copy the resulting Config ID into NEXT_PUBLIC_HUME_CONFIG_ID in .env.local, then restart the dev server.
